gauge.name: Cuivre River at Troy

impacts.statement: Substantial flood damages begin in mobile homes on the southeast side of Tropicana Village Mobile Home Park in Moscow Mills.
Water begins to affect the US 61 northbound pavement at the Cuivre River bridge north of Troy.
The southbound lane of US Highway 61 north of Troy begins to flood just south of the bridge.
Water begins surrounding several homes along Tobago Court in Tropicana Village Mobile Home Park in Moscow Mills.
Vehicle storage lot starts flooding on the north end of Tropicana Village Mobile Home Park in Moscow Mills.
Near this height, MoDoT will consider closing U.S. Highway 61 at the Cuivre River bridge north of Troy in both directions.
Moderate flooding begins...significant agricultural land is inundated.
Near this height...Second Street in Moscow Mills begins flooding near the Byrd Street intersection.
Near this height, State Highway H near the West Fork Cuivre River bridge will be closed due to flooding.
Gravens Road begins flooding north of Highway C.
Water begins covering Frenchman Bluff Road (County Road 658) just west of the Cuivre River bridge.
Considerable agricultural land is flooded between Troy and Moscow Mills.
From Highway 61 east to Frenchmans Bluff... low lying agricultural land is inundated.

Moscow Bridge Road just east of Moscow Mills looking south on July 31, 2008 at a stage near 23.5 ft.
Cropland south of Highway C and one mile east of Moscow Mills on July 31, 2008 at 24.5 ft.
Storage lot in Tropicana Village in north Moscow Mills on October 9, 2009 with TRYM7 near 26.9 ft.

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
